title: ConvertAction
description: An action that converts a resource from one representation or format to another.
$comment:
This is a Stencila extension of schema.org [`Action`](./action.md).

It represents format, encoding, packaging, or document-model conversion. In
Stencila, rendering an executable document can be represented as an
[`ExecuteAction`](./execute-action.md) followed by a `ConvertAction`, rather
than as a separate render-specific action. In graphs, conversion actions are
usually attached to the resource-flow edge that links the source to the
converted result.

analogues.notes: Base schema.org action type extended for conversion-specific provenance.

analogues.name: C2PA c2pa.converted action
analogues.notes: Close C2PA action projection when the file format or representation changed.

analogues.name: PROV wasDerivedFrom
analogues.notes: Approximate provenance analogue for conversion-derived results.

type.$comment: This is a special property analogous to JSON-LD's `@type` keyword.

id.$comment: This is a special property analogous to JSON-LD's `@id` keyword.

identifiers.description: Any kind of identifier for any kind of Thing.
identifiers.$comment:
Some identifiers have specific properties e.g the `issn` property for
the `Periodical` type. These should be used in preference to this
property which is intended for identifiers that do not yet have a
specific property. Identifiers can be represented as strings, but
using a `PropertyValue` will usually be better because it allows
for `propertyID` (i.e. the type of identifier).

actionStatus.description: The current status of the action.

agent.description: The direct performer or driver of the action.

participants.description: Other agents that participated in the action.

provider.description: The service provider, service operator, or performer responsible for the action.

objects.description: The objects or input values upon which the action is carried out.

results.description: The objects or values produced by the action.

instrument.description: The object, software, or other instrument that helped perform the action.

environment.description: Environment variables or settings that affected the action.
environment.$comment:
This follows the Workflow Run RO-Crate pattern of representing execution
environment settings as `PropertyValue` values attached to an action.
Use this for settings that are part of the runtime environment rather
than ordinary command-line or document parameters.

containerImages.description: Container images used by the action.
containerImages.$comment:
This follows the Workflow Run RO-Crate `containerImage` property. A string
value may be used when only an image URI or locator is known; a
`ContainerImage` value should be used when registry, tag, or digest
metadata is available.

error.description: An error produced by the action.
